message:
fix(pthread,stdlib): cond_wait owner check + abort SIGABRT unblock

bd-cie59: pthread_cond_wait/timedwait now verify the calling thread
owns the mutex before clearing the recursive/errorcheck owner+count
bookkeeping. Without this check, a thread that incorrectly invokes
cond_wait without holding the mutex would clobber the real owner's
state and silently steal the mutex on wake. Returns EPERM for both
RECURSIVE and ERRORCHECK when the caller is not the owner; matches
glibc semantics. Also bundles a small native_pthread_join self-join
detection by-tid fallback.

bd-r25ks: abort() now unblocks SIGABRT in the calling thread before
each raise via rt_sigprocmask(SIG_UNBLOCK, {SIGABRT}). POSIX requires
"abort() shall first unblock or unignore the SIGABRT signal." Without
this, a process that has SIGABRT blocked never receives the default
core-dump action — production crashes lose their core file and the
SIG_DFL re-raise path is bypassed entirely.

Regression test condvar_wait_errorcheck_without_lock_returns_eperm
pins the new behavior. Full abi suite: 2974/2974 pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
